calibration.stated_target: close stuck-vs-idle Pattern conflation in window-empty sparkline sub-case; secondary fixes for freshest+failed double-encoding and same-agent multi-thread chip collision

hud-fidelity.rationale: The targeted refinement lands cleanly. The case-(ii) `·Nm` suffix is information-bearing ink, not chrome: it is strictly conditional (suppressed in cases i and iii where the sparkline shape itself carries the answer), Tier-3 dim so it sits visually beneath the cells, ASCII-only (zero codepoint cost), bounded at 4 cells, and explicitly framed as a 'degenerate-case disambiguator' rather than a label like the seed's '7/min'. Row footprint 8 + 1 + 4 = 13 cells inside a 28-cell content area. It reads as a status-line-field primitive within the sparkline row — not a second instrument — because it appears only when the sparkline conveys no rate, which is the exact opposite of double-encoding. The §Sparkline contract's prose ('the case-(ii) suffix is not a rate label — it is a degenerate-case disambiguator that only renders when the shape carries no rate at all') is precisely the Tufte argument I would have demanded. The dropped-gutter-on-error rule (rule a) is the cleanest move in the iteration: when col 1 already carries `SEV_ERROR`, the col 0 `●` is suppressed, because freshest+failed is already pinned by 'topmost severity glyph in the stripe' + top-row position + Tier-2 description weight (mirroring chip mode's identical fallback). 'One mark per signal' is preserved across freshest, failed, and freshest+failed — the same recovery pattern (position + tier weight) covers every gutter-forfeit case, which is the kind of unifying rule Tufte asks for. The same-agent multi-thread tie-breaker is a clean O(n) fall-through to eyebrow mode and prevents the `pi│ pi│` chip-collision that would have collapsed identity. Per-row primitive count audit: sparkline-row case-i = 1 (sparkline only), case-ii = 2 (sparkline + status-line-field suffix), case-iii = 1; eyebrow row = 1 (status-line-field source); activity row eyebrow-mode = 3 (gutter + verb-glyph + description); activity row chip-mode = 3 (chip-prefix + verb-glyph + description); activity row freshest+failed = 2 (severity-glyph + description, gutter dropped). All ≤3. No italic, no decorative chrome, no restated info. Demerits keeping this off 10: (1) the column-1 precedence stack is now five rules deep (system-tag → error+gutter-drop → verb-tag → regex-classifier → blank) plus an orthogonal chip/eyebrow precedence with a same-agent tie-breaker — the rules are individually justified but collectively read-the-spec rather than perceive-the-rule, which is Tufte-acceptable but not Tufte-clean. (2) The colour discipline still has one unstated edge: §Verb-glyph rule 1 strips the `(failed)` text suffix and leans on `SEV_ERROR` for exception, but the candidate never pins how `tone` composes with the Tier-2/Tier-3 description weight on the description text itself — does a freshest+failed row render description as 'Tier-2 weight + tone red,' or does tone bypass tiers like severity glyphs do? The shipped behaviour is tone-coloured descriptions, but the new tier-weight rule doesn't say which wins on overlap. This is the only remaining hole in the colour spec.

tests.spellcheck_test: Pass — verb-glyph in col 1, severity-glyph displacement on failed rows, freshest gutter on row 0, all on the line of work itself; no popup, no side pane.
tests.sparkline_test: Pass — 8 cells, fixed `▁▂▃▄▅▆▇█` alphabet, inside an existing line of the band; geometry pinned at 8 cells × 8 s/bucket = 64 s window with explicit `Math.ceil(7 * count / max)` mapping; case-(ii) suffix is a status-line annotation on the same row, not a second sparkline.

four-questions.rationale: Iter 3's stated job is to close the last remaining gap — pattern in the window-empty sub-case where flat sparkline conflated 'wedged' (recent burst then silence) with 'idle' (no events to date) — and it does. Liveness: sparkline existence + non-flat shape (active case) or `·Nm` suffix presence (case ii) signals 'something happened recently or is happening now'; flat-only (case i) signals 'nothing has ever happened.' Pattern: in active case the sparkline shape carries burst/flat/converging/decaying directly (state 4 mock shows the failure-cascade decay shape `▆▆▇▆▅▄▃▂` reading as 'high then tailing off' without reading); in window-empty case the `·Nm` suffix carries 'how long since the last event' as a 4-char glance ( `·45s` paused, `·2m` briefly stuck, `·15m` silent, `·1h`/`·3d` likely abandoned); in no-logs case the absence of suffix is itself the signal. Pattern is now glance-perceptible across all three sparkline sub-cases, which is the gap iter 2 left open. Exception: col-1 SEV_ERROR displacement creates a vertical red stripe under load (state 4 mock: 4 of 5 rows red), and gutter-mass exception count is the canonical Tufte 'count without reading' move. The single surviving non-error glyph in row 2 of state 4 is itself diagnostic ('this one passed') — small-multiples discipline working as intended. Latest: gutter `●` on freshest in eyebrow mode; in chip mode and freshest+failed the gutter forfeits but latest is recovered by top-row position + Tier-2 description weight on row 0 (same fallback in both edge cases — consistent rule, not ad-hoc patch). Glance-readability of `·Nm` is comparable to vim/tmux status-line duration fields — 4-char tokens parse pre-attentively in trained users, well within the 'glance not read' bar.

per_question_grade.liveness: answered by sparkline existence + shape (active) or suffix presence (window-empty)
per_question_grade.pattern: answered by sparkline shape in active case; by `·Nm` suffix duration in window-empty case (closes iter-2 gap); by suffix absence in no-logs case
per_question_grade.exception: answered by col-1 SEV_ERROR vertical stripe (gutter mass)
per_question_grade.latest: answered by gutter `●` on freshest, with consistent position + Tier-2 fallback in chip mode and freshest+failed mode

terminal-feasibility.rationale: Glyph budget: exactly 6 new glyphs (1 freshest gutter `nf-md-record` U+F05CB + 5 verb glyphs U+F0208/F0279/F0349/F03EB/F040A), all in MDI PUA range U+F0000–U+F1AFF guaranteed single-cell EAW Neutral by the nerd-fonts patcher. The seed's EAW-Ambiguous `●` U+25CF and EAW-Wide `⚠` U+26A0 are explicitly retired; the candidate has done the EAW homework. The `·Nm` suffix introduces zero new codepoints — pure ASCII (`·` U+00B7 + digits + s/m/h/d), with explicit fallback to `.` documented if `·` proves unreliable in a future terminal. The chip separator `│` U+2502 is EAW Neutral and already used by codebase box-drawing. All four states have mockups at exactly 30 cols (the tightest realistic width) — sparkline 8 cells fits in 28-cell content, chip mode `pi│ ` + pad + verb + pad leaves 24 cells for description (pre-truncated with `…`), eyebrow mode is a single Tier-4 row before a multi-row run. Refresh contract is pinned (logs[] mutation + 1 Hz tick driven by existing focused-session timer — no new timer infrastructure). Y-axis scaling is given as `Math.ceil(7 * count / Math.max(localMax, 1))` — division-by-zero guarded explicitly. Producer-side `verb?` tag is correctly flagged SHOULD-not-MUST with a renderer-side regex-classifier fallback (`/^Reading /` → read, etc.) so the design ships today regardless of producer changes. `splitOutcome()` bridge to the existing shipped renderer is cited with file:line (`apps/tui/src/index.tsx:210`) and the regex is reproduced — minimum spec for someone implementing this. System-tag rule 0 reuses existing severity-glyph entries (`[bell]` → `nf-md-bell-alert`) so the budget of 6 covers everything. Two open questions remain (heading absorption colour-only-vs-glyph; gutter gradient under low-volume) but both are explicitly deferred to user testing with documented defaults — not blockers. Loss to a 10: (1) the `·` U+00B7 EAW-Ambiguous fallback rationale, while documented, is the one remaining glyph-width assumption — it relies on iTerm2/Alacritty/kitty/wezterm using single-width policy, which is plausible but not bulletproof across user environments; (2) the description-tier × tone composition rule is unstated (see hud-fidelity demerit), which is a renderer-side spec hole an implementor would have to ask about; (3) two open questions still exist, even if non-blocking.

states_covered.empty: two sub-cases mocked (i no-logs, ii window-empty) at 30 cols
states_covered.multi_source: state 3 mocked at 30 cols, chip mode operating across pi/cc interleave
states_covered.error_heavy: state 4 mocked at 30 cols, SEV_ERROR vertical stripe with rule (a) gutter-drop on freshest+failed shown explicitly
states_covered.narrow_30_cols: all four mocks already at 30 cols — no separate narrow mock needed

notes_for_phase_2: If another lens proposes simplifying the column-1 precedence stack (rule 0 system-tag → rule 1 error+gutter-drop → rule 2 verb-tag → rule 3 regex → rule 4 blank), my ASI is non-conflicting — the colour-composition rule applies regardless of how the column-1 displacement is decided.

Secondary Tufte concern not in the ASI: the air-row decision is case-by-case (state 2 has one between sparkline and eyebrow; state 3 has none because chip column self-divides). A 10/10 spec would pin air-row as a first-class rule ('air row separates sparkline-band from activity-band when the latter starts with an eyebrow; chip mode supplies its own divider'). Smaller than the colour-composition gap, deferred.

Tertiary: the case-(ii) `·Nm` suffix lands left-aligned (immediately after the sparkline cells) rather than right-aligned to the content-area edge. At 30 cols this is correct (right-aligned would leave 15 cells of awkward whitespace between sparkline and suffix); at 60-col widths it would still be correct because the sparkline-as-leading-anchor reads naturally with the suffix adjacent. No change needed, but worth noting if a panelist proposes the alternative.

Quaternary: the candidate retires the seed's row-end `✓`/`✗` badges entirely. Worth confirming in synthesis — implicit success (no glyph) is the right Tufte default, but if any other lens raises 'how do I see a successful test pass land?' the answer is 'tone-coloured (passed) suffix from `splitOutcome()` is preserved per §Verb-glyph rule 1 last sentence' — already in the spec, just easy to miss.

Calibration: my read of 9/10/9 (avg 9.33) is the strongest yet across iterations, fitting the orchestrator's narrative that iter 3 closes the last four-questions gap. The hud-fidelity score holds at 9 because the layered precedence rules (necessary complexity to handle four states cleanly + system-tags + multi-source interleave) are read-the-spec, not perceive-the-rule. A pure 10 on this lens would require simpler primitives, but simpler primitives can't carry the four-states-cleanly burden — this is a Pareto frontier the candidate sits near.
